query: Set up GitHub CLI on this Windows machine and make future terminal publishing to GitHub work end to end.
This skill repo is local only right now. Create the GitHub repo, push main, and publish a release with the packaged .skill asset.
This folder is nested inside another git repository. Publish only this subfolder to GitHub and do not touch the parent repo.
gh auth login keeps failing on Windows. Use the most reliable path and get this machine ready to publish repos anyway.
Repair origin for this local repo and push the current branch to the correct GitHub repository.
Create a release for this repository and upload two .skill files as assets.
Before touching GitHub, show me a JSON plan for how you would publish this folder and whether you need a clean publish repo.
Automatically ship this Windows skill repo to GitHub, including repo creation, push, and release publishing.
Use a fallback path if git push or gh release is flaky. I still need the repo and release published today.
Publish this local tool folder to GitHub with HTTPS remotes and upload the packaged asset after the push.
Review this GitHub Actions workflow and tell me whether the release job is safe.
Teach me how gh repo view, gh pr create, and gh issue list work.
Rewrite this README so it looks more polished and product focused.
Publish this npm package to npmjs and update the changelog.
Write a PowerShell script that sorts zip files into folders by month.
Do a code review of this git repository and focus on the latest frontend changes.
I already created the GitHub release in the browser. Just help me improve the release notes text.
Install Node.js, pnpm, and a few VS Code extensions on this machine.
Deploy this private documentation site to Vercel with environment variables and a custom domain.
Write a beginner tutorial explaining git, GitHub, fork, branch, and origin.
